name: Curie Mobile Component Manifest
description: Machine-readable catalog of the mobile counterpart of every Curie desktop component. One entry per desktop component (ids match components-manifest.json). Styling lives in responsive.css (the .m-* system, active <=600px); snippets are rendered inside a 390px phone frame with a pg- body class. Fields mirror the desktop manifest plus: chrome (render outside .m-view), mobileTreatment, desktopEquivalent.

components.name: Side Nav
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the bottom .m-tabbar + .m-drawer
components.purpose: The mobile primary navigation. The fixed left sidebar collapses into a fixed bottom tab bar (.m-tabbar) of five top-level destinations — Home, Briefs, Games, Editorial, Newsletter — with the current section marked by full opacity. Secondary/profile items (Preferences, Logout) move into the hamburger .m-drawer opened from the app bar.
components.whenToUse: On every mobile page as the persistent primary nav. Give the current section's tab is-active. Overflow destinations and profile actions belong in the .m-drawer, not the tab bar.
components.whenNotToUse: Never inside the content column — it is fixed chrome. Don't exceed five tabs; put additional destinations in the drawer.
components.interactions: Fixed at the bottom on a blurred translucent surface; each .m-tab navigates to its section and the current one carries is-active (rendered by opacity, not colour). The app-bar hamburger opens the .m-drawer overlay for profile and overflow items. Touch has no :hover — active state is the only affordance.

components.name: Top Banner
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the fixed .m-appbar
components.purpose: The fixed mobile top app bar — the mobile stand-in for the desktop top banner. Logo or back button on the left, centered page title with its heading icon, and a hamburger (list pages) or nothing (detail pages) on the right.
components.whenToUse: On every mobile page as the persistent header. Use the boxed back button variant on detail pages and the hamburger on list pages.
components.whenNotToUse: Never inside content — it is fixed chrome. Don't stack two of them.
components.interactions: Fixed at the top with a blurred translucent surface. The hamburger opens the .m-drawer; the boxed back button navigates up. No :hover on touch.
components.snippet: Briefs

components.name: Page Header
components.mobileTreatment: Folded into the .m-appbar as the per-page heading bar
components.purpose: The list-page heading variant of the fixed .m-appbar: the Curie logo on the left, the section's title paired with its heading icon centered, and the hamburger on the right. It carries the desktop page-header's job — naming the current section — into the mobile top bar.
components.whenToUse: As the header on a mobile list/section page (Briefs, Games, Podcasts, Newsletter). Swap the .m-appbar__icon and the title text to match the section.
components.whenNotToUse: On the Editorial section (use editorial-header) or on detail pages, which use the boxed back-button variant instead of the hamburger.
components.interactions: Fixed and blurred at the top of the viewport. The hamburger opens the .m-drawer nav; the whole bar stays pinned while the content column scrolls beneath it. No :hover on touch.
components.snippet: Games

components.name: Editorial Header
components.mobileTreatment: Editorial variant of the .m-appbar with a boxed back button
components.purpose: The Editorial section's fixed top bar: a boxed back button (.m-appbar__btn--boxed) and logo on the left, the media icon paired with the 'Editorial' title centered, and the hamburger on the right. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop editorial-header.
components.whenToUse: On mobile Editorial pages — especially editorial-detail, where the boxed back button returns to the Editorial list.
components.whenNotToUse: On non-Editorial sections (use page-header) or where no upward navigation exists (drop the boxed back button).
components.interactions: Fixed and blurred at the top. The boxed back button navigates up to the Editorial list; the hamburger opens the .m-drawer. The boxed button gets a subtle bordered surface so it reads as tappable. No :hover on touch.
components.snippet: Editorial

components.name: Footer
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the .m-footer-cta big footer call-to-action
components.purpose: The big footer call-to-action that closes out the mobile home page: a centered headline (with an accent span), a short supporting line, and a full-width primary button. It stands in for the desktop footer as the terminal block of the mobile content column.
components.whenToUse: At the very bottom of the mobile home content, inside .m-view, to end the page on a single conversion prompt (e.g. app download).
components.whenNotToUse: For in-content messaging or when the page already carries a competing CTA; keep it to one per page as the closing block.
components.interactions: Sits in the content column (not fixed chrome). The full-width .btn--primary is the single tap target; the accent span in the title is decorative only. No :hover on touch.
components.snippet:
Turn spare minutes into clinical confidence.

Join thousands of clinicians who rely on Curie's quick news and games to stay ahead in their field.

components.name: Editorial Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-lcard — thumbnail on top, title + teaser + author row below
components.purpose: The mobile entry point to a long-form editorial piece: a full-width stacked card with the thumbnail on top and the title, one-line teaser, and an author/read-more row below. It replaces the desktop 3-up .editorial-grid card once the viewport drops under 600px.
components.whenToUse: For the vertically stacked Editorial list on mobile (editorial.html), where each long-form feature gets one full-width card.
components.whenNotToUse: For dense scannable briefs (use latest-card / related-story-card) or the circular Curie-Brief rail (use brief-item / m-brief).
components.interactions: The whole card is an anchor to the editorial detail page. On touch there is no hover; the tinted glass surface is static.
components.snippet:
Rising Voices in Oncology

A curated look at the oncologists shaping the next era of cancer care — through…

By Curie Team Read more

components.name: Brief Item
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-brief — circular thumbnail with play badge in a horizontal snap rail
components.purpose: The compact, scannable brief tile for the mobile 'Curie Briefs' rail: a circular thumbnail ringed in purple with a play/document badge, then a date, a truncated title, and the source name. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop brief-item, reshaped into a circle for the horizontal snap rail.
components.whenToUse: Inside a horizontally scrolling .m-hscroll rail on Home (index.html), where an HCP swipes through many briefs fast.
components.whenNotToUse: For the full vertical briefs feed (use latest-card) or featured/hero placements (use m-fcard).
components.interactions: Each tile scroll-snaps to the rail's start edge and taps through to the brief detail. No hover on touch; the play badge is a visual affordance, not a separate control.
components.snippet:
Jul 22, 2026

ARPA-H funds $25.8M gene-editing platfor…

news-medical.net

components.name: Podcast Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-fcard with the --pod badge + cta (podcast variant of the featured card)
components.purpose: The mobile audio-episode tile: the shared featured card (.m-fcard) carrying the purple PODCAST badge over the artwork and a purple 'Listen Now' cta below the date, title, and teaser. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop podcast card.
components.whenToUse: For podcast episodes in the mobile Featured Content rail or a podcast list, where audio content needs a play affordance.
components.whenNotToUse: For written briefs (use the --news variant) or games (use game-card / the --game variant).
components.interactions: The cta triggers playback; the whole card taps through to the episode. No hover on touch. In a rail the card scroll-snaps at 84% width.
components.snippet:
PODCAST
Dec 2, 2025

What an Active Shooter Taught an Oncologist

Dr. Beatrice Preti reflects on fear, resilience, and caring for patients with life-limiting…

components.name: Game Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-fcard with the --game badge + cta (game variant of the featured card)
components.purpose: The mobile game tile: the shared featured card (.m-fcard) carrying the pink GAME badge over the illustration and a pink 'Play Now' cta below the date, title, and description. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop game card in the Home featured rail.
components.whenToUse: For a Curie game surfaced in the mobile Featured Content rail on Home (index.html); the Games hub itself stacks the verbatim .game-card instead.
components.whenNotToUse: For written or audio content (use the --news or --pod variants).
components.interactions: The cta launches the game; the whole card taps through. No hover on touch. In a rail the card scroll-snaps at 84% width.
components.snippet:
GAME
Jul 22, 2026

Daily Connections

Exercise your brain with this addictive word association…

components.name: Profile Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .edp centered profile card (page-scoped in editorial-detail.html — not .m-lcard or .profile-card)
components.purpose: The mobile KOL / featured-expert card as it renders on the editorial detail page: a single-column, centre-aligned card with a ringed round avatar, name, institution, a specialty tag pill, social links, and a short bio. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop profile-card, restyled centre-aligned for the phone canvas.
components.whenToUse: On the mobile editorial detail page (editorial-detail.html) to introduce each featured oncologist in the stacked '2026 List'.
components.whenNotToUse: For linking to a story or article (use related-story-card / editorial-card) or for any non-person entity.
components.interactions: The social links open the expert's X / LinkedIn; the card body itself is not a link. No hover on touch.
components.snippet:
Marla Lipsyc-Sharf
UCLA Health
Breast Oncology

One of the few voices actively focused on breast cancer in young women — her research on fertility, treatment timing, pregnancy after diagnosis, and circulating tumor DNA monitoring is filling a long-neglected gap in the field.

components.name: Related Story Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-lcard nested inside the .m-related-block on the detail page
components.purpose: The compact related-read tile on the mobile brief detail page: a full-width .m-lcard (thumbnail on top, title, and a source + time footer) stacked inside a single tinted 'Related stories' block. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop related-story-card.
components.whenToUse: In the 'Related stories' block at the foot of a mobile brief or editorial detail page (brief-detail.html) to surface recommended reads.
components.whenNotToUse: As the primary row of a full briefs feed (use latest-card) or to profile a person (use profile-card).
components.interactions: Each card is an anchor to the related brief; the surrounding block is a static container. No hover on touch.
components.snippet:
Related stories

Recommended reads based on this brief

JAK inhibitors carry a meaningfully higher cancer risk than TNF inhibitors across…
Medscapeabout 10 hours ago

components.name: Latest Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-lcard with meta + the shared .actions bar (read, bookmark, vote, share)
components.purpose: The primary feed row for the mobile 'Latest' briefs list: a full-width .m-lcard with the thumbnail on top and the title (as a link), a time + source meta line, and the shared .actions bar below. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop latest-card.
components.whenToUse: For each item in the vertically stacked 'Latest' briefs feed on the mobile briefs list page (briefs.html).
components.whenNotToUse: For compact related-reads tiles in a detail page (use related-story-card) or the circular Home rail (use brief-item).
components.interactions: The card carries data-href and taps through to the detail page; taps inside the .actions bar (read / bookmark / upvote / downvote / share) are handled separately and don't navigate. No hover on touch.
components.snippet:
Clover Health discloses data breach after social engineering attack on employee accounts

about 16 hours ago
Fierce Healthcare

components.name: Brief Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-lcard — media on top, body below
components.purpose: The mobile list card for a brief or story: a full-width stacked card with the image on top and the title, meta, and action bar below. It replaces the desktop side-by-side brief card once the viewport drops below 600px.
components.whenToUse: For any vertical feed of briefs / stories on mobile — the Briefs list, Editorial list, and related-stories rails all stack these.
components.whenNotToUse: For the horizontally-scrolled Featured rail (use m-fcard) or the circular Curie-Brief rail (use m-brief).
components.interactions: The whole card is the tap target to the detail page; taps on the inner action bar (.actions) are excluded from navigation. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Clover Health discloses data breach after social engineering attack
Fierce Healthcareabout 16 hours ago

components.name: Brief Detail Hero Image
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-detail__hero — full-width rounded lead image
components.purpose: The lead visual at the top of a mobile brief detail page: a full-width, rounded 16:10 hero image that sits directly above the title. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop brief-detail-image block.
components.whenToUse: As the single lead image at the top of a brief (or editorial) detail page on mobile, before the title and meta row.
components.whenNotToUse: For thumbnails inside feed or related cards (those use m-lcard__media / m-fcard__media) — this is only the one large hero.
components.interactions: Purely presentational; the image is not a tap target. Fixed 16:10 aspect ratio so the crop stays consistent across briefs. No hover on touch.
components.snippet: Clover Health discloses data breach after social engineering attack on employee accounts

components.name: Filter Dropdown
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the .m-filterbar ("For You" pill + "Filters" pill), backed by the .m-sheet bottom sheet
components.purpose: The mobile list-filtering control: a top-of-feed bar holding a "For You" pill (which reveals a small For You / Latest menu) and a "Filters" pill that opens the full filters bottom sheet. It is the mobile stand-in for the desktop label + value + chevron filter dropdown.
components.whenToUse: At the top of any browse/list feed on mobile (Briefs, Editorial) where an HCP narrows by specialty, indication, or resource type.
components.whenNotToUse: For single on/off toggles or page navigation. When switching only between two peer views of the same stream, use the segmented toggle instead.
components.interactions: Tapping the "For You" m-pill opens the m-foryou__menu (For You / Latest); tapping the "Filters" m-pill opens the .m-sheet filters bottom sheet, where a specialty select plus Clear All / Apply live. No hover state on touch.

components.name: Segmented Toggle
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas (shared .seg-toggle from components.css)
components.purpose: A two-option segmented control with an animated sliding indicator for switching between mutually exclusive peer views of the same content stream. It has no dedicated .m-* variant — the desktop control is compact enough to sit unchanged in the mobile content column.
components.whenToUse: To switch between a small fixed set (2-3) of peer views of one stream on mobile, such as 'For You' vs 'Latest' on a feed.
components.whenNotToUse: For more than 3 options, for navigation between pages, or for multi-select filtering (use the mobile filter bar / bottom sheet instead).
components.interactions: Tapping a button activates it and slides the __slider beneath it; JS toggles .active and translates the slider. Exactly one button is active at a time. No hover state on touch.

components.name: Form Field
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-field — stacked label + input
components.purpose: The atomic unit of Curie mobile forms: a full-width labelled text input on the mobile form surface, with a rose required marker on the label. It replaces the desktop form-group / form-input pairing once the viewport drops below 600px.
components.whenToUse: Any mobile capture flow — the newsletter signup and preferences forms stack these inside an .m-form card.
components.whenNotToUse: For filtering or looking up existing content (use the mobile search field) or for a single fixed choice (use the .m-field__select).
components.interactions: Full-bleed 16px input on a translucent surface; tapping focuses it and raises the mobile keyboard. Touch has no hover; the required asterisk in the label span is rose.

components.name: Error Input Field
components.mobileTreatment: Reuses the desktop .form-input.is-error + .form-error row inside an .m-field
components.purpose: A mobile form field in its invalid state — the rose error surface and border on the control paired with an inline form-error message that names the fix. There is no dedicated .m-field error skin, so the field wraps the desktop error building blocks unchanged.
components.whenToUse: Whenever a mobile field fails validation and the HCP must correct it before submitting (a malformed NPI or email, an unselected required option).
components.whenNotToUse: For neutral guidance or page-level / transient feedback (use a toast). For the message row on its own, see form-error.
components.interactions: Add .is-error to the reused .form-input / .form-select to switch it to the rose error surface, then render a .form-error row directly beneath. Remove both once the field becomes valid. No hover on touch.
components.snippet: Enter a valid 10-digit NPI number

components.name: Inline Newsletter Bar
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-newsletter subscribe card
components.purpose: The mobile newsletter-subscription prompt: a rounded gradient card with an envelope icon, headline, supporting line, and a full-width Subscribe button. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop inline newsletter bar.
components.whenToUse: To surface a low-friction Weekly Dose signup inline between content sections on mobile home and editorial feeds, without interrupting the reading flow.
components.whenNotToUse: As the primary conversion destination that captures name/email/specialty (use the mobile signup form) or when only an implicit email opt-in is needed.
components.interactions: Tapping the full-width m-newsletter__btn starts the free subscription flow. The card is an otherwise static inline promo. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Stay current in minutes.
All the week's key medical news in one email.

components.name: Newsletter Signup Card
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-form + .m-field newsletter form
components.purpose: The self-contained mobile newsletter signup form: a rounded card with a Playfair title, supporting line, stacked name / email / specialty fields, and a full-width submit button. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop signup card.
components.whenToUse: As the primary conversion surface on the dedicated mobile newsletter page, where HCP name, email, and specialty are collected.
components.whenNotToUse: Inline within a busy feed (use the m-newsletter bar) or when only an implicit email opt-in is required.
components.interactions: The HCP types name and email and picks a specialty; required fields are flagged with a red asterisk span. Tapping m-form__submit submits the subscription. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Get Curie's Weekly Dose

Free. Weekly. Quick to read.

components.name: Button
components.mobileTreatment: Full-width primary CTAs (.m-newsletter__btn / .m-fcard__cta / .m-form__submit) plus the .m-pill
components.purpose: The mobile action control. Loud actions become full-width solid-purple CTAs sized for the thumb — the subscribe button on the newsletter card, the coloured Read Now / Play Now button on a Featured card, and the form submit. The compact filter control is the rounded .m-pill. Every skin is grounded in the golden mobile pages, not the desktop pill scale.
components.whenToUse: Use a full-width CTA (.m-newsletter__btn, .m-form__submit, or the .m-fcard__cta --game/--news/--pod skin) for the one loud gesture of a card or view. Use the .m-pill for a compact secondary control that sits in a row, such as the For You selector or Filters on the Briefs bar.
components.whenNotToUse: For icon-only actions on a card (use the Icon Button action bar) or for inline navigation inside prose (use the Link). Don't stack two full-width primary CTAs in the same card — keep one loud gesture.
components.interactions: Full-width CTAs fill the content column and centre their label (with an optional leading icon). Touch has no :hover — the press is the only feedback. The .m-pill toggles a menu / sheet; give menu pills aria-haspopup and aria-expanded.
components.snippet:
<button class="m-newsletter__btn" type="button">Subscribe free</button>
<button class="m-fcard__cta m-fcard__cta--news" type="button" style="margin-top:16px">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><line x1="4" y1="8" x2="14" y2="8"/><line x1="4" y1="12" x2="14" y2="12"/><line x1="4" y1="16" x2="10" y2="16"/></svg>
  Read Now
</button>
<button class="m-pill" type="button" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false" style="margin-top:16px">For You
  <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="6,9 12,15 18,9"/></svg>
</button>

components.name: Content Badge
components.mobileTreatment: Reused as the .m-fcard__badge card tag + .m-fcard__date eyebrow
components.purpose: The type + date eyebrow that identifies what a piece of content is before the reader commits. On mobile the type half becomes the .m-fcard__badge corner tag on the card media (tinted --news / --game / --pod) and the date sits in the card body as .m-fcard__date — together they carry the same 'what + when' signal as the desktop content-badge.
components.whenToUse: On featured / hero cards on the mobile home and editorial views where the content type isn't obvious from its surrounding rail.
components.whenNotToUse: Inside dense mobile lists where the section heading already declares the type.
components.interactions: Purely informational — the badge and date are static; only the card's CTA is interactive.
components.snippet:
BRIEF
Apr 13, 2026

Two surgical approaches for bone metastases show timing advantages

components.name: User Avatar
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A compact circular initials chip identifying the signed-in user, optionally paired with the user's full name. There is no mobile-specific variant; the desktop .user-avatar (and its .user-name label) are reused as-is for the account identity block — e.g. in the drawer header or a profile row on the mobile canvas.
components.whenToUse: To represent the current user on a mobile view — inside the drawer's account row or a settings header, with the adjacent .user-name when horizontal room allows.
components.whenNotToUse: For decorative or multi-person avatars (use avatar / avatar-group), or where a real profile photo is required — this is an initials-only chip.
components.interactions: Static identity indicator; non-interactive in the golden reference and unchanged on touch.
components.snippet:
mr
moumitra roychowdhury

components.name: Editorial Hero
components.mobileTreatment: Page-local .ed-hero card built on .m-detail__title
components.purpose: The mobile editorial feature hero: a rounded card with a photographic background, dark gradient overlay, an eyebrow (Curie Editorial pill + date), an accented title, a description, stacked Subscribe / Share actions, and reading metadata. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop editorial hero.
components.whenToUse: At the top of a mobile editorial feature or detail page to establish topic, set tone, and offer the primary subscribe/share actions.
components.whenNotToUse: On list or home feeds, or where no photographic background / editorial framing is appropriate.
components.interactions: Static hero; the stacked full-width Subscribe (btn--primary) and Share (btn--border) actions are the tap targets. Background and overlay are decorative layers. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Curie Editorial March 2026

Rising Voices inOncology

A curated look at the oncologists shaping the next era of cancer care — through research, advocacy, and digital influence.

5 min read 10 Voices Featured

components.name: Home Hero Banner Carousel
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the Featured Content .m-hscroll rail of .m-fcard cards
components.purpose: The desktop home hero carousel has no direct mobile equivalent; on the mobile home the featured content it rotated through is surfaced as a horizontally-scrolled 'Featured Content' rail of themed feature cards (brief / game / podcast / news), each with a media image, content-type badge, date, title, description, and a themed CTA.
components.whenToUse: At the top of the mobile home feed to spotlight featured content across content types, where the badge/CTA color encodes the content type.
components.whenNotToUse: On detail or list pages (use the editorial hero), or when a single static feature needs highlighting rather than a scannable rail.
components.interactions: The rail scrolls horizontally with scroll-snap; each card's themed m-fcard__cta opens that piece of content. No auto-rotation and no hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Featured Content

Jul 22, 2026

ARPA-H funds $25.8M gene-editing platform t…

Massachusetts General Hospital lands a major federal grant to figh…

GAME
Jul 22, 2026

Daily Connections

components.name: Almanac Meeting Card
components.mobileTreatment: No dedicated mobile design — reused via .m-summary on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: Triages an upcoming medical conference for an HCP on mobile: the meeting name in the summary head, a priority-tier and specialty-domain badge row, an Editor's Take blurb with date and location, and Track / View-details actions. Composed from the detail-page .m-summary card since the almanac has no dedicated mobile block.
components.whenToUse: In a mobile 'Conferences to track' list where an HCP scans upcoming congresses and decides which are worth their time — one .m-summary per meeting.
components.whenNotToUse: For a podcast recap of a conference or any non-event content — use the relevant content card (m-lcard / m-fcard).
components.interactions: Track and View-details are the tap targets; the card body itself is not navigable. No hover state on touch — the buttons rely on their pressed/active feedback.
components.snippet:
ESC Congress 2026
Must-track Cardiology

Editor's take: The field-defining late-breakers land here — expect practice-changing read-outs across heart failure and lipid-lowering therapy. Aug 29 – Sep 1 · Amsterdam, NL.

components.name: Conferences to Track Card
components.mobileTreatment: Reuses the desktop .almanac-card, full-width on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: The mobile card for the home page's "Conferences to track" (Almanac) section: a full-width row that triages a medical meeting for HCPs — a compact date block, a must-track tier badge + specialty domain, the meeting name, date/location meta, an editor's take, and Track / View details actions.
components.whenToUse: In the mobile "Conferences to track" list to help an HCP decide which meetings are worth their calendar. Lead with the tier badge and the editor's take — that's the triage value.
components.whenNotToUse: For a tool (use the Curie Tools Card) or a news/editorial item (use a card). Not for a generic event with no editorial judgment attached.
components.interactions: Track toggles a follow on the meeting; View details opens the agenda. No hover dependency — both actions are tap targets on mobile.
components.snippet:
SEP
12
–16
MUST-TRACK Translational neurology

ANA 2026 · American Neurological Association Annual Meeting

Sep 12–16, 2026 Baltimore, MD
EDITOR'S TAKE

The one neurology meeting where practice-changing trials actually debut — clear your calendar for the late-breakers.

View details

components.name: AI Tool Card
components.mobileTreatment: No dedicated mobile design — reused via .m-fcard (body-only, no media) on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A launcher tile for an AI-powered clinical utility on mobile: title, description, optional capability tags, and a full-width primary CTA, composed from the featured-card .m-fcard body since Curie Tools have no dedicated mobile block.
components.whenToUse: In a mobile 'Curie Tools' list where an HCP picks a productivity tool (handout generation, prior-auth drafting, drug lookup) — one .m-fcard per tool.
components.whenNotToUse: For content (briefs, editorials, podcasts) — use the relevant content card. Tags are optional and may be dropped for simpler tools.
components.interactions: The full-width CTA is the primary tap target that launches the tool. No hover state on touch; the CTA gives pressed feedback.
components.snippet:
Patient Handout Generator
Turn any Curie brief into a plain-language, patient-ready handout in seconds — checked against the source before you print.
Handouts Source-cited

components.name: Curie Tools Card
components.mobileTreatment: Reuses the desktop .ai-tool-card, full-width and single-column on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: The mobile launcher tile for a Curie AI clinical tool (Patient Handout Generator, Prior Authorization, PeerRx Drug Lookup). On the home page's "Curie Tools" section the desktop 3-up .ai-tools-grid collapses to a single full-width column, and each .ai-tool-card renders as a tall stacked tile: icon, title, description, capability tags, a timing/source meta line, and a full-width launch button.
components.whenToUse: In the mobile "Curie Tools" rail to surface an AI utility the HCP can launch. Keep the tags to the two or three that matter and the meta line to the speed/source promise.
components.whenNotToUse: For editorial or news content (use a card), or for a conference (use the Conferences to Track Card). Not a plain button — it carries the tool's identity and trust signals.
components.interactions: The whole card lifts on hover on desktop; on touch the launch button is the tap target. The button spans the card width for an easy thumb target.
components.snippet:
Patient Handout Generator

Generate a patient-ready handout for any regimen in seconds — written in plain language and sourced from approved labeling.

Regimen-specificFDA-sourcedFor oncologists
<60 seconds · Built from FDA-approved labeling

components.name: Game Mode Card
components.mobileTreatment: No dedicated mobile design — reused via .m-fcard (game variant) on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: Presents one mode of a learning game on mobile: a color-coded mode badge, title, description, a compact time / learning-points / credits line, and a prominent Play Now CTA. Composed from the .m-fcard game variant (pink badge + pink CTA) since game modes have no dedicated mobile block.
components.whenToUse: In a mobile 'More ways to play' list to present the modes of a game (matching, myth-or-bust, confidence) with reward context — one .m-fcard per mode.
components.whenNotToUse: For the illustration-led game launcher (use the stacked game-card) or the compact quick-play tile (see game-tile).
components.interactions: The Play Now CTA is the tap target that starts the mode. No hover state on touch; the CTA gives pressed feedback.
components.snippet:
Myth or Bust
Cardiology Myth or Bust
Sort fact from fiction across 10 rapid cardiology claims and see where the evidence actually lands.
~4 min · 120 LP · 0.25 credits

components.name: Game Tile
components.mobileTreatment: No compact mobile tile — folds into the stacked .game-card inside .m-stack
components.purpose: The compact desktop quick-play tile has no compact mobile counterpart: on mobile the Games page stacks the full .game-card (title, illustration, description, Play) in an .m-stack, so quick-play collapses into the same stacked launcher.
components.whenToUse: For a mobile games feed — reuse the stacked .game-card in .m-stack; there is no narrow quick-play rail at phone width.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not try to reproduce the desktop square tile grid on mobile; the horizontal quick-play rail is dropped in favor of the single-column stack.
components.interactions: Each card's Play button is the tap target; on the Games page the card is scaled up (body.pg-games) for a larger illustration. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Curie Connections

Find the hidden connections among this mix of medical terms.

components.name: Coming Soon Note
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas — full-width
components.purpose: A full-width dashed-border notice bar that closes out a list, signalling more content is on the way. There is no mobile-specific variant; the desktop .coming-soon-note is reused as-is at the foot of the mobile games .m-stack, spanning the content column.
components.whenToUse: At the end of a mobile grid or feed (e.g. below the stacked game cards on games.html) to set the expectation that the list will grow.
components.whenNotToUse: For actionable empty states that need a CTA, or for per-item status (use a badge).
components.interactions: Static, non-interactive — unchanged on touch.
components.snippet: More games coming soon — check back for new challenges!

components.name: Multi-select Chips
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged inside an .m-field
components.purpose: A pick-many control that shows selected values as removable purple chips inside an input-styled field with a chevron. It reuses the desktop .multiselect verbatim, dropped into an .m-field so it reads as one mobile form.
components.whenToUse: When an HCP selects several values from a set (therapeutic areas, specialties) on mobile and needs to see and remove each selection.
components.whenNotToUse: For a single choice (use the .m-field__select) or an on/off state (use the toggle).
components.interactions: Tapping the field opens the option menu (product); each chip's × removes that value. The field wraps chips onto multiple lines as they accumulate. No hover on touch.
components.snippet: Oncology Neurology

components.name: Toggle Switch
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A single on/off switch for a boolean preference with an adjacent label, reused verbatim from the desktop component. It stacks in the mobile newsletter preferences list.
components.whenToUse: For opt-in / opt-out preferences on mobile (briefing notifications, weekly digest). Add .is-on for the on state, .is-disabled for a locked control.
components.whenNotToUse: For choosing among options (use the radio group) or multiple selections (use multiselect).
components.interactions: Tapping the track flips it and slides the knob; the on state fills the track purple. Disabled dims the control and label. The full 44px row is a comfortable touch target.
components.snippet:
Daily briefing notifications
Weekly digest email

components.name: Radio Group
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A pick-one control for a small set of options, each optionally carrying a supporting description, reused verbatim from the desktop component. Full-width rows stack cleanly in the mobile form.
components.whenToUse: For mutually-exclusive choices on mobile (newsletter frequency, role). Mark the chosen option's row with .is-selected.
components.whenNotToUse: For many/searchable options (use the .m-field__select) or multi-select (use multiselect).
components.interactions: Tapping a row moves the filled purple ring to it. Each row is a full-width touch target. Can be paired with a form-error row for group validation.

components.name: Field Error Message
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: An inline validation row — a rose alert icon plus a short message — shown beneath an errored mobile field. Reused verbatim from the desktop component.
components.whenToUse: Directly under any mobile form control in its error state (input, select, multiselect, radio) to explain what needs fixing. Pair with .is-error on the control.
components.whenNotToUse: For page-level or transient feedback (use a toast) or neutral help text.
components.interactions: Static; appears and disappears with the field's validity. Uses the --color-error rose. No interaction of its own.
components.snippet: Enter a valid 10-digit NPI number

components.name: Toast Notification
components.mobileTreatment: Reused, full-width on mobile
components.purpose: The same transient status message as the desktop toast — success, error, warning, or info with an accent icon disc, title/message, and close affordance — reused unchanged on the mobile canvas where it stretches to the full content width.
components.whenToUse: For feedback on a mobile action or system event (subscribe confirmed, save failed, offline, new brief ready). It spans the content column edge-to-edge inside .m-view.
components.whenNotToUse: For field-level validation (use form-error) or persistent in-context notices (use inline-alert).
components.interactions: Auto-dismisses or is tapped closed via toast__close; there is no hover on touch. The disc color and glyph encode the semantic — teal success, rose error, amber warning, blue info.
components.snippet:
Subscribed
Check your inbox to confirm.

components.name: Modal
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the bottom sheet
components.purpose: On mobile the centered desktop dialog is replaced by the .m-sheet bottom sheet — a panel that slides up from the bottom edge over a dimmed overlay, with a header, title, close, body content, and a footer action row. It focuses the user on a single decision or a compact set of controls (e.g. the Briefs filters).
components.whenToUse: To interrupt the mobile flow for a short, self-contained task — filters, a confirmation, or a focused form. Toggle .is-open to reveal it.
components.whenNotToUse: For transient, non-blocking notices (use a toast) or for content large enough to warrant its own page.
components.interactions: Tapping the trigger adds .is-open; the panel transforms up from translateY(100%) as the overlay fades in. Tapping the overlay or the close button dismisses it, and the footer holds the Clear/Apply (or Cancel/Confirm) buttons. No hover on touch.
components.snippet:
Filters
Specialty

components.name: Accordion
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas — full-width
components.purpose: A vertical stack of expandable items that disclose their panel content one section at a time. There is no mobile-specific accordion, so the desktop .accordion is reused as-is inside the mobile content column, spanning the full width of the 390px canvas.
components.whenToUse: To compress long, scannable content such as FAQs or grouped detail into collapsible sections on a mobile detail or form page, where vertical space is scarce.
components.whenNotToUse: When every passage must stay visible at once, or when there is only one short block that never needs collapsing.
components.interactions: Tapping a trigger toggles .is-open on its item, rotating the chevron and revealing the panel. Touch has no :hover, so the desktop hover lift never fires on mobile — only the open/closed state matters.
components.snippet:
Each brief distills the most relevant studies, approvals, and commentary for your specialty into a five-minute read.

Briefs refresh every morning before 7am in your local time zone.

components.name: Tooltip
components.mobileTreatment: Hover tooltip becomes a tap popover (.m-sources__menu)
components.purpose: Because touch has no hover, the desktop hover tooltip becomes a tap-to-open popover on mobile. It is grounded in the brief-detail .m-sources__menu pattern — a small token-styled panel anchored to its trigger that discloses a short supplemental note or list when tapped.
components.whenToUse: To reveal a brief hint or a short list (e.g. the sources behind a brief) from an inline trigger that can't rely on hover.
components.whenNotToUse: For essential always-visible content, long passages, or a full command list (use the bottom sheet or a dedicated page).
components.interactions: Tapping the trigger toggles .is-open on the menu, which anchors just below the trigger; tapping elsewhere closes it. There is no hover reveal on touch — the popover is pinned open by tap.
components.snippet: 1 Source Fierce Healthcare

components.name: Dropdown Menu
components.mobileTreatment: Anchored .m-foryou pill + .m-foryou__menu
components.purpose: A pill trigger that opens a small floating list of selectable options beneath it. On the Briefs list it is the 'For You' feed switcher (For You / Latest); the same pattern appears as the 'Sources' menu on a brief detail.
components.whenToUse: For a compact set of mutually-exclusive choices anchored to a control in the content column — a feed sort, a saved view, a small option list.
components.whenNotToUse: For a large or scrolling set of filter fields (use the Filters bottom sheet .m-sheet) or for primary navigation (use the .m-tabbar).
components.interactions: Tapping the .m-pill toggles is-open on the .m-foryou__menu and flips aria-expanded; the current choice carries is-active (filled purple). Tapping an item selects it and closes the menu; a tap outside dismisses it. No hover state on touch.

components.name: Tabs
components.mobileTreatment: The app-level bottom .m-tabbar; in-content switchers collapse to the .m-foryou dropdown
components.purpose: On mobile the tab metaphor is carried by the fixed bottom tab bar (.m-tabbar), which switches between the app's top-level sections. There is no in-content tab strip on mobile — a peer-view switcher like the desktop For-You/Latest segmented toggle collapses into the .m-foryou dropdown instead.
components.whenToUse: As the app-level view switcher across sections. For a small in-content choice of peer views, use the .m-foryou dropdown rather than a horizontal tab strip that would crowd the 390px width.
components.whenNotToUse: Do not build a horizontal in-content tab row on mobile — labels crush below 600px. Do not place it in the content column; the tab bar is fixed chrome.
components.interactions: Fixed at the bottom on a blurred surface; the active section's .m-tab carries is-active (opacity). Selecting a tab navigates to that section's view. Touch has no :hover, so opacity is the only selected affordance.

components.name: Progress
components.mobileTreatment: Reading-progress bar pinned under the app bar (.m-progress)
components.purpose: On a mobile detail page the determinate progress concept becomes the reading-progress bar (.m-progress) — a thin fill nested inside the .m-appbar and pinned to its bottom edge, growing left-to-right as the reader scrolls. The labelled desktop .progress bar (progress__track/progress__fill) is still reused for in-content determinate values.
components.whenToUse: As the reading-position indicator on brief/editorial detail pages; or reuse .progress in the content column when a labelled completion percentage is known.
components.whenNotToUse: For indeterminate waits with no measurable progress (use a spinner) or a single done/not-done boolean.
components.interactions: Non-interactive. The .m-progress fill width is driven by scroll position and eases via a 0.1s linear width transition, carrying the DS teal-to-blue progress gradient.
components.snippet: Curie Brief

components.name: Avatar
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A compact circular representation of a person via their photo or an initials fallback, alone or stacked in a group. There is no mobile-specific avatar; the desktop .avatar sizes are reused as-is — e.g. in the drawer identity header or a profile block on the mobile canvas.
components.whenToUse: To identify an author, member, or participant on a mobile view, and to stack a group to imply a set of collaborators where horizontal room allows.
components.whenNotToUse: For the signed-in-user identity chip (use user-avatar), for non-person entities better shown by an icon, or where a full name label is required for clarity.
components.interactions: The desktop hover lift/glow and the group's raise-on-hover do not fire on touch. On mobile these render as static identity marks; the group still overlaps to imply a set.
components.snippet:
MR JD
MR +4

components.name: Badge
components.mobileTreatment: Mobile card badge .m-fcard__badge; shared .badge reused as-is
components.purpose: A small pill that labels or categorizes content. On mobile cards the real badge is .m-fcard__badge — an absolutely-placed corner pill on the featured-card media, tinted by content type (--game / --news / --pod). The shared desktop .badge tint pills are also reused unchanged for inline status and category tags.
components.whenToUse: Use .m-fcard__badge to stamp a content type onto a featured-card image (BRIEF, GAME, PODCAST); use the shared .badge tint variants inline to tag status, specialty, or phase.
components.whenNotToUse: For interactive actions best served by a button, or for long phrases that would break the pill shape.
components.interactions: Both the card badge and the shared tint pills are static labels — no tap or hover behaviour on the mobile canvas.
components.snippet:
BRIEF
Oncology New Phase III

components.name: Icon Button
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged — the same .actions / .act / .votes / .vbtn row, plus the boxed .m-appbar__btn
components.purpose: The compact icon-only actions on every mobile brief and story card — mark read, bookmark, like / dislike, and share. The exact desktop .act circles and .votes / .vbtn segmented vote pill are reused unchanged on the mobile canvas. The boxed .m-appbar__btn is the mobile-only square icon button used for the app-bar back / menu affordance.
components.whenToUse: For the per-card action bar (read · bookmark · vote · share) on any mobile list or detail card, reusing the same markup so every card reads as one system. Use the boxed .m-appbar__btn for a single square glyph action such as the detail-page back button.
components.whenNotToUse: When the action needs a visible text label to be understood, or for the loud primary gesture of a view (use a full-width Button). Never ship an icon button without an accessible name.
components.interactions: Touch has no :hover, so the sentiment tints (read teal, bmark gold, share blue, up green, dn red) are seen mainly on the desktop breakpoint; on mobile the press is the feedback. Bookmark / vote arrows use the brand assets BookMark.svg, up arrow.svg, and down arrow.svg. Every control needs an aria-label.

components.name: Checkbox
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A single binary control for opt-in choices and multi-select lists, reused verbatim from the desktop component. It styles a real native checkbox so keyboard and assistive-tech behaviour come for free while the box picks up the Curie purple fill.
components.whenToUse: For independent on/off choices and lists where more than one option can be picked on mobile. Wrap the input and its text in a single label.checkbox so the whole row is tappable.
components.whenNotToUse: For mutually exclusive options (use the radio group) or an immediate settings switch (use the toggle).
components.interactions: :checked fills the box purple with a white check; .is-indeterminate shows a purple dash; :disabled dims the control and mutes the label. The whole label row is the touch target.

components.name: Textarea
components.mobileTreatment: Reused full-width inside an .m-field
components.purpose: A multiline text input on the Curie form surface for longer free-text — notes, feedback, message bodies. The desktop .textarea is reused full-width inside an .m-field so it matches the single-line mobile inputs.
components.whenToUse: When a mobile response can run to multiple lines or paragraphs. Keep it vertically resizable so users can expand for longer answers.
components.whenNotToUse: For short single-line values (use the .m-field input) or fixed-option choices (use the select / checkbox / radio).
components.interactions: :focus brightens the surface with a purple border and ring; .is-error paints the rose surface and red border; :disabled dims and stops resizing. No hover on touch.

components.name: Search Input
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A pill-shaped search field with a leading magnifier and an optional clear button, for filtering lists of briefs, podcasts, or editorial items on mobile. Reused verbatim from the desktop component, full-width on the mobile canvas.
components.whenToUse: Any time mobile content can be filtered or looked up by keyword. Show the clear button once the field has a value so users can reset in one tap.
components.whenNotToUse: For general free-text entry (use the .m-field input) or multi-line text (use the textarea). Not a substitute for the Filters bottom sheet when facets are needed.
components.interactions: The whole wrapper brightens with a purple border and ring on focus-within, raising the mobile keyboard. The clear button resets the field. .is-disabled dims the wrapper and blocks input.

components.name: Select
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-field__select
components.purpose: The mobile styled dropdown for choosing one value from a known list — the .m-field__select wraps a real native <select> so it keeps the native mobile picker under the Curie surface. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop select, used for Specialty on the newsletter form.
components.whenToUse: For a single choice from a moderate, known set of options on mobile (specialty, sort order), stacked inside an .m-field with its label.
components.whenNotToUse: For a handful of visible options where radios read better, for boolean settings (use the toggle), or for very large searchable datasets.
components.interactions: Tapping the control opens the native mobile option picker. The disabled selected option acts as the placeholder. No hover on touch.

components.name: Link
components.mobileTreatment: Purpose-built inline affordances — .m-lcard__more, .m-section__link, .m-detail__sources
components.purpose: The mobile inline links that carry the reader forward: the purple Read more arrow that closes a list card (.m-lcard__more), the See all / View All section link in a section head (.m-section__link), and the tappable Sources chevron on a detail page (.m-detail__sources). They replace the desktop inline .lnk with layouts and hit areas sized for touch.
components.whenToUse: Use .m-lcard__more as the forward affordance inside a stacked list card, .m-section__link in a section head to jump to the full list, and .m-detail__sources to reveal the source list on a brief / editorial detail page.
components.whenNotToUse: For the loud primary gesture of a view (use a full-width Button) or for the per-card icon action bar (use the Icon Button). Keep these quiet forward cues; don't scatter many in one block.
components.interactions: Touch has no :hover — the purple accent and trailing glyph (arrow, chevron-right, chevron-down) are the affordance. .m-detail__sources toggles the sources menu, so give it role="button", aria-haspopup, and aria-expanded.
components.snippet: Read more See all 1 Source

components.name: Spinner
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: The same rotating accent ring as the desktop spinner, reused as-is on mobile to signal an indeterminate wait — content loading, a request in flight, or a background action processing. Available in small, medium, and large sizes.
components.whenToUse: For short, indeterminate mobile waits where progress can't be shown — inside buttons, cards, or overlays while data resolves.
components.whenNotToUse: When real progress can be shown (use progress), when a whole view takes more than a few seconds (use skeleton), or as decoration.
components.interactions: Non-interactive. Spins continuously via a CSS keyframes rotation at a steady linear cadence until removed from the DOM.

components.name: Skeleton
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: The same shimmer placeholder as the desktop skeleton, reused on mobile to mirror the shape of content still loading and prevent layout shift. Here it fills the footprint of a loading .m-lcard list card — a media rectangle above stacked text lines.
components.whenToUse: While a mobile view or card is fetching, laid in the same footprint the real content will occupy — e.g. a Briefs/Editorial list card placeholder.
components.whenNotToUse: For very short waits (use spinner), a truly empty result (use empty-state), or as a permanent stand-in for missing data.
components.interactions: Non-interactive. A highlight band sweeps left-to-right on a looping shimmer keyframes animation until the real content replaces it.

components.name: Empty State
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: The same centered explain-and-guide block as the desktop empty state, reused on mobile inside the content column. It explains why a list, search, or collection has nothing to show and offers a next action.
components.whenToUse: When a mobile list, search, or collection legitimately has nothing to show — first-run, cleared filters, or zero results — and you can point the user forward.
components.whenNotToUse: While content is still loading (use skeleton or spinner) or to communicate an error (use inline-alert).
components.interactions: The optional action is a composed .btn that responds to tap (no hover on touch); the container itself is static.
components.snippet:
No saved briefs yet

Briefs you bookmark will appear here for offline reading. Start with today's briefing.

components.name: Inline Alert
components.mobileTreatment: Reused, full-width on mobile
components.purpose: The same in-context banner as the desktop inline alert — info, success, warning, or error with an icon, title/message, and optional close — reused on the mobile canvas where it spans the full content width, anchored next to the content it concerns.
components.whenToUse: To give persistent, in-context feedback tied to a mobile section or form, using the semantic variant that matches the message's severity.
components.whenNotToUse: For transient, dismiss-on-timeout notifications (use a toast) or for empty regions (use empty-state).
components.interactions: Static banner. The optional .alert__close button dismisses the alert on tap; there is no hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
Heads up
This dataset was last refreshed 6 hours ago.

components.name: Divider
components.mobileTreatment: Dedicated .m-divider 1px rule
components.purpose: A hairline horizontal separator that groups stacked content in the mobile content column — a 1px translucent rule with generous vertical margins. It is the mobile counterpart of the desktop divider.
components.whenToUse: To separate stacked sections inside a mobile detail view, such as between the action bar and the summary card on a brief detail page.
components.whenNotToUse: To box or frame content (use a card), and avoid stacking multiple dividers where whitespace alone would read cleaner.
components.interactions: Non-interactive presentational separator; no states and no hover.
components.snippet:
Fierce Healthcare · about 16 hours ago
Two surgical approaches for femoral bone metastases show timing-based advantages.

components.name: Table
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by stacked key/value rows
components.purpose: There is no mobile table — columns cannot breathe at 390px. Each record collapses into its own full-width .m-lcard, with the leading field as the card title and every remaining column rendered as a label/value row (.m-lcard__foot with two .m-lcard__by spans, space-between). The reader scrolls a stack of records instead of scanning a grid.
components.whenToUse: When genuinely tabular desktop content (leaderboards, metric breakdowns, dosage or roster tables) has to survive on mobile — stack one card per row so each record stays legible.
components.whenNotToUse: For a single key/value pair (inline it) or for content that already reads well as a feed of cards (use m-lcard directly).
components.interactions: Static — no row-hover tint on touch. Each card is a self-contained record; scanning is vertical rather than across columns.
components.snippet:
Belantamab
PhasePhase III
Readouts12
Teclistamab
PhasePhase II
Readouts8

components.name: Pagination
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by a load-more button
components.purpose: Numbered page controls do not survive the mobile width. Instead a paged list is walked with a single full-width 'View More' load-more button at the foot of the feed, which appends the next batch in place.
components.whenToUse: At the bottom of any long mobile feed (Briefs, Editorial, Games) to fetch and append the next page of items without leaving the view.
components.whenNotToUse: When the whole set already fits, or when the list is short — omit it entirely. Do not attempt to render a numbered pagination row on mobile.
components.interactions: Tapping the button loads and appends the next batch, keeping scroll position; it hides once the list is exhausted. The subline gives context beneath it. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet: Explore all the latest healthcare news and insights

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.mobileTreatment: Replaced by the app-bar back button
components.purpose: A multi-crumb hierarchy trail is too wide for a phone, so on mobile it collapses to a single boxed back button (.m-appbar__btn--boxed) sitting on the left of the detail app bar — one tap back up the hierarchy rather than a full path.
components.whenToUse: On any mobile detail page as the up-navigation affordance, in place of the desktop breadcrumb. Label it for its parent (e.g. 'Back to Briefs').
components.whenNotToUse: On top-level list pages, which show the hamburger instead. Don't try to render an ancestor path on mobile.
components.interactions: Tapping navigates one level up to the parent list. The boxed variant adds a faint surface stroke so the target reads as a control; it keeps a 44px minimum touch target. No hover state on touch.
components.snippet:
<button class="m-appbar__btn m-appbar__btn--boxed" type="button" aria-label="Back to Briefs">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="15 5 8 12 15 19"/></svg>
</button>

components.name: Stepper
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas (desktop .stepper)
components.purpose: A horizontal progress indicator for a multi-step flow — numbered markers joined by connector lines showing which steps are done (teal check), which is current (purple ring), and which are still ahead. There is no dedicated mobile design; the desktop .stepper is placed directly in the mobile content column and reads well at three short steps.
components.whenToUse: On a mobile wizard or multi-step form to show how many steps there are and where the user stands. Keep to a few steps with short labels so the row fits the ~340px content width.
components.whenNotToUse: When the labels cannot fit the phone width or the flow has many steps — collapse to a simple progress bar instead. Don't use it for free navigation (use tabs).
components.interactions: Static in the reference: markers and connectors reflect flow state via is-complete / is-active / is-upcoming. Completed connectors fill teal; the active marker carries a purple ring. Advance is-active and flip finished steps to is-complete as the user progresses.
components.snippet:
Account
2 Preferences
3 Review

components.name: Slider
components.mobileTreatment: Reused unchanged on the mobile canvas
components.purpose: A range control for picking a value along a continuum — a thin track with a blue-to-green fill up to the current value and a white, purple-ringed thumb. Reused verbatim from the desktop component, full-width on mobile.
components.whenToUse: When a single value is chosen from a bounded continuous range on mobile and position matters more than the exact number — volume, playback scrubbing, a dosage window. Set the fill width and thumb left to the current value.
components.whenNotToUse: For precise numeric entry (use a number input), for a few discrete options (use radios), or when the range is unbounded.
components.interactions: Dragging the thumb along the track sets the value; the fill width and thumb left are the only inline values, driven by the current position. .is-disabled dims the whole control and blocks interaction.
